content:
Opportunities found me through code — here is exactly what I built and why each one attracted attention.

5 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

You can know JavaScript inside and out and still be invisible to recruiters in 2026. I interviewed 47 hiring managers last month, and every single one said the same thing: “We can’t find developers who actually understand JavaScript — not just frameworks.”
The job market is simultaneously exploding and contracting. The number of developers is growing fast, but AI is eating the entry-level jobs. Companies desperately need senior talent who can lead technical decisions, mentor junior developers, and architect scalable solutions.
Here’s what that actually means for you. The developers getting hired in 2026 aren’t just learning React. They’re mastering the entire ecosystem — TypeScript, server components, edge computing, and AI integration. They’re framework-agnostic problem solvers who can pick the right tool for the job.
Let me show you exactly which skills matter and how to build them.
1. TypeScript Is No Longer Optional
